1	[Task frontend-test-coverage-002-execute-impl-child-1] PHASE 3a: PLAN
2	Title: Test context/state providers (AssumptionContext + SessionContext)
3	
4	Read .fleet/tasks/frontend-test-coverage-002-execute-impl-child-1/questions.json and .fleet/tasks/frontend-test-coverage-002-execute-impl-child-1/research.json
5	for context on what was asked and answered.
6	
7	YOU MUST create .fleet/tasks/frontend-test-coverage-002-execute-impl-child-1/plan.json with this structure:
8	{
9	  "phase": "plan",
10	  "decisions": [
11	    { "id": "d1", "decision": "...", "based_on": ["q1"], "alternatives_rejected": ["..."], "reversibility": "easy|medium|hard" }
12	  ],
13	  "steps": [
14	    { "step": 1, "task": "...", "depends_on": [] }
15	  ],
16	  "spawn_children": false,
17	  "children": []
18	}
19	
20	DO NOT STOP until plan.json exists and contains at least one step.
21	Verify: ls -la .fleet/tasks/frontend-test-coverage-002-execute-impl-child-1/ to confirm the file was written.
22	
23	DECOMPOSITION: Set spawn_children=true for independent sub-tasks that warrant
24	their own 4-phase workflow. Format: { "id": "...", "title": "...", "description": "..." }
25	
26	BEFORE YOU STOP: git add -A && git commit --no-verify -m "plan: frontend-test-coverage-002-execute-impl-child-1"

content.thinking:
Now I have all the context I need. Let me create the plan. The research confirmed:

1. No test files exist for either context - need to write from scratch
2. AssumptionContext API: addAssumption (with dedup by variableId), removeAssumption, clearAll, hasAssumption, getAssumption, contextPayload, useAssumptions throw without provider
3. SessionContext API: loadSession (from localStorage), saveSession (to localStorage), setAccountId, setAgentId, isConfigured (accountId.length > 0), corrupt localStorage handling, default empty values, useSession throw without provider


4. Session type has accountId and agentId as strings
5. The existing tests use vitest and @testing-library/react, and I'll need to use renderHook from @testing-library/react for testing these custom hooks.

content.text: I have all the context needed. Now I'll create the plan.
